question: Implement a `Car` class that exhibits composition with an `Engine` class. The `Engine` should be created and destroyed along with the `Car` object. Demonstrate this lifetime management.

test_cases.expected:
--- Demonstrating Readability / Efficiency Issue ---

Creating a luxurious sports car.
[Engine Lifecycle]: Engine SPRT-E9000 is being constructed now.
[Car Lifecycle]: Car Speedster X (Red) object created. Engine SPRT-E9000 has been installed.
--- Car Details ---
Model: Speedster X
Color: Red
Engine Serial: SPRT-E9000
-------------------
[Car Operation]: Car Speedster X (Red) is preparing to drive.
[Engine Operation]: Engine SPRT-E9000 initiating start sequence...
[Car Operation]: Car Speedster X is now moving.
[Car Status]: Querying engine status for Car Speedster X.
[Engine Maintenance]: Performing routine check on Engine SPRT-E9000.

Creating a standard family sedan.
[Engine Lifecycle]: Engine FAM-E5000 is being constructed now.
[Car Lifecycle]: Car Family Cruiser (Blue) object created. Engine FAM-E5000 has been installed.
--- Car Details ---
Model: Family Cruiser
Color: Blue
Engine Serial: FAM-E5000
-------------------
[Car Operation]: Car Family Cruiser (Blue) is preparing to drive.
[Engine Operation]: Engine FAM-E5000 initiating start sequence...
[Car Operation]: Car Family Cruiser is now moving.

Objects going out of scope.
[Car Lifecycle]: Car Family Cruiser (Blue) object is being destructed. Associated Engine FAM-E5000 will be destroyed.
[Engine Lifecycle]: Engine FAM-E5000 is being destructed now.
[Car Lifecycle]: Car Speedster X (Red) object is being destructed. Associated Engine SPRT-E9000 will be destroyed.
[Engine Lifecycle]: Engine SPRT-E9000 is being destructed now.

errors: Using `std::unique_ptr<Engine>` for composition when a direct member variable (`Engine engine;`) would achieve the same strict ownership and lifetime management more simply, efficiently (avoiding heap allocation for the `Engine` object itself and indirection overhead), and idiomatically for direct composition.

Excessive and overly verbose logging messages (`[Engine Lifecycle]:`, `[Car Operation]:`, `--- Car Details ---`) for demonstrating basic object lifetime, which reduces readability and clutters output.
Unnecessary member function `logCarDetails()` which only prints object state, often better handled by overloading `operator<<` or integrating into `drive()`/`checkEngineStatus()` if truly necessary.
